questionText: Which design pattern does the following code implement?
explanation: One class offering a simple method that orchestrates several subsystem objects is a Facade.

questionText: Complete the State pattern: Connection delegates behavior to its current ConnectionState.
explanation: The context holds a state object, can replace it, and forwards requests to it (passing itself for transitions).

questionText: For each pattern, pick the characteristic you would use to recognize it in code.
matchingOptions: Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory
A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from
The context delegates behavior to a swappable state object that it can replace at runtime
Each handler holds a reference to the next handler and forwards what it cannot handle
A single class delegates to several subsystem classes to hide their complexity
Relies on a clone()/copy operation to duplicate a pre-configured object

correctAnswers.Chain of Responsibility: Each handler holds a reference to the next handler and forwards what it cannot handle
correctAnswers.Facade: A single class delegates to several subsystem classes to hide their complexity
correctAnswers.Flyweight: Splits object data into shared intrinsic state and per-context extrinsic state, often cached in a factory
correctAnswers.Memento: A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from
correctAnswers.Prototype: Relies on a clone()/copy operation to duplicate a pre-configured object
correctAnswers.State: The context delegates behavior to a swappable state object that it can replace at runtime

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which SOLID principle does the following code violate?
explanation: Invoice mixes business calculation, persistence and printing — three separate reasons to change.

questionText: Complete the Composite where Group holds children uniformly with Circle.
explanation: Both leaf and composite implement the component type; the composite recurses the same operation over its children.

questionText: Match each design pattern to the structural/behavioral trait that identifies it.
matchingOptions: The context holds an algorithm interface reference that can be set/replaced at runtime
Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
A single class delegates to several subsystem classes to hide their complexity
Uses a fluent chain of setter-style methods ending in a build() call
A wrapper that implements the same interface as the object it wraps and adds behavior around it

correctAnswers.Adapter: Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
correctAnswers.Builder: Uses a fluent chain of setter-style methods ending in a build() call
correctAnswers.Decorator: A wrapper that implements the same interface as the object it wraps and adds behavior around it
correctAnswers.Facade: A single class delegates to several subsystem classes to hide their complexity
correctAnswers.Mediator: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
correctAnswers.Strategy: The context holds an algorithm interface reference that can be set/replaced at runtime

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which SOLID principle does the following code violate?
explanation: Adding a new shape forces editing this method; it is not closed for modification.

questionText: Complete the Composite where Folder holds children uniformly with FileLeaf.
explanation: Both leaf and composite implement the component type; the composite recurses the same operation over its children.

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their telltale implementation characteristic.
matchingOptions: Uses a fluent chain of setter-style methods ending in a build() call
Subjects keep a list of subscribers and call an update method on each when they change
A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements
The context holds an algorithm interface reference that can be set/replaced at runtime

correctAnswers.Adapter: Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
correctAnswers.Builder: Uses a fluent chain of setter-style methods ending in a build() call
correctAnswers.Factory Method: A creator class declares an abstract factory method that subclasses override
correctAnswers.Iterator: Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements
correctAnswers.Observer: Subjects keep a list of subscribers and call an update method on each when they change
correctAnswers.Strategy: The context holds an algorithm interface reference that can be set/replaced at runtime

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Which SOLID principle does the following code violate?
explanation: An Ostrich cannot be substituted for a Bird because it breaks the inherited fly() contract.

questionText: Complete the Composite where Menu holds children uniformly with MenuItem.
explanation: Both leaf and composite implement the component type; the composite recurses the same operation over its children.

questionText: For each pattern, pick the characteristic you would use to recognize it in code.
matchingOptions: Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in
Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements
An object implementing the same interface as the real subject intercepts calls to it

correctAnswers.Adapter: Wraps an existing class and translates its calls to a target interface
correctAnswers.Iterator: Exposes hasNext() and next() to walk a collection's elements
correctAnswers.Proxy: An object implementing the same interface as the real subject intercepts calls to it
correctAnswers.Template Method: A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: Match each design pattern to the structural/behavioral trait that identifies it.
matchingOptions: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
Uses a private constructor and a static accessor so only one object ever exists
The context holds an algorithm interface reference that can be set/replaced at runtime
Relies on a clone()/copy operation to duplicate a pre-configured object

correctAnswers.Mediator: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

questionText: For each pattern, pick the characteristic you would use to recognize it in code.
matchingOptions: A factory interface declares one creation method per product in a related family
Each handler holds a reference to the next handler and forwards what it cannot handle
Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in

correctAnswers.Chain of Responsibility: Each handler holds a reference to the next handler and forwards what it cannot handle
correctAnswers.Mediator: Colleague objects send events to a central object instead of calling each other
correctAnswers.Template Method: A final base method calls abstract step methods that subclasses fill in

questionText: Match each design pattern to the structural/behavioral trait that identifies it.
matchingOptions: A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from
Holds a reference to a separate implementation hierarchy chosen at construction time
An object implementing the same interface as the real subject intercepts calls to it
Elements expose accept(visitor) and call back visit(this) for double dispatch

correctAnswers.Bridge: Holds a reference to a separate implementation hierarchy chosen at construction time
correctAnswers.Memento: A separate snapshot object stores state that an originator can later restore from
correctAnswers.Proxy: An object implementing the same interface as the real subject intercepts calls to it
correctAnswers.Visitor: Elements expose accept(visitor) and call back visit(this) for double dispatch

explanation: Each option is the recognizable hallmark of that pattern.

explanation: Each setter returns this for chaining; build() constructs the enclosing product from the builder.

questionText: Match each SOLID principle to its definition.
matchingOptions: A class should have only one reason to change
Subtypes must be substitutable for their base types without breaking behavior
High-level modules should depend on abstractions, not on concrete low-level modules
Software entities should be open for extension but closed for modification
Clients should not be forced to depend on methods they do not use

correctAnswers.Single Responsibility Principle (SRP): A class should have only one reason to change
correctAnswers.Open/Closed Principle (OCP): Software entities should be open for extension but closed for modification
correctAnswers.Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP): Subtypes must be substitutable for their base types without breaking behavior
correctAnswers.Interface Segregation Principle (ISP): Clients should not be forced to depend on methods they do not use
correctAnswers.Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP): High-level modules should depend on abstractions, not on concrete low-level modules

explanation: Standard SOLID definitions.

questionText: Which change best fixes the Open/Closed violation in a draw() method that uses 'if (shape instanceof Circle) ... else if (shape instanceof Square) ...'? Select 1 or more answers
options: Give each shape a draw() method and call shape.draw() polymorphically
Define an abstract Shape with an abstract draw() that each subclass implements
Add another else-if branch for every new shape
Use a giant switch on a shape-type string instead of instanceof
Make the method longer but keep the type checks
correctAnswers: Give each shape a draw() method and call shape.draw() polymorphically
Define an abstract Shape with an abstract draw() that each subclass implements
explanation: OCP is satisfied by polymorphism: new shapes extend the hierarchy without editing existing code. More branches/switches keep violating it.

questionText: A Service class does 'new MySqlRepository()' internally. Which change best restores the Dependency Inversion Principle?
options: Inject a Repository abstraction through the constructor
Move the new MySqlRepository() call into a static block
correctAnswers: Inject a Repository abstraction through the constructor
explanation: DIP: depend on an injected abstraction, not a concrete class created internally.

questionText: Match each SOLID principle to a code situation that violates it.
matchingOptions: Adding a new shape forces you to edit an existing switch statement that draws shapes
A SimplePrinter forced to implement scan() and fax() with empty bodies
A high-level OrderService that directly instantiates a concrete MySqlDatabase
A class that both generates a report and also emails it and writes it to disk
A Square subclass of Rectangle breaks code that sets width and height independently

correctAnswers.SRP: A class that both generates a report and also emails it and writes it to disk
correctAnswers.OCP: Adding a new shape forces you to edit an existing switch statement that draws shapes
correctAnswers.LSP: A Square subclass of Rectangle breaks code that sets width and height independently
correctAnswers.ISP: A SimplePrinter forced to implement scan() and fax() with empty bodies
correctAnswers.DIP: A high-level OrderService that directly instantiates a concrete MySqlDatabase

explanation: Each scenario is a textbook violation of the matched principle.
